sourceMovie.title: RoboCop
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: RoboCop (1987) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paul Verhoeven's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If RoboCop clicked for you, Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If RoboCop clicked for you, Insurgent (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after RoboCop?
faq.answer: Try Terminator 2: Judgment Day, Insurgent, Upgrade—each shares DNA with RoboCop while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is RoboCop?
faq.answer: RoboCop (1987) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is RoboCop part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "RoboCop Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
